# Indirect Coordination

## Explains

Explains why traces left by one entity naturally coordinate the work of others without explicit communication.

## The Concept

Stigmergy is the mechanism by which traces left in the environment by one entity coordinate the behavior of others without direct communication. Ants do not talk to each other — they leave pheromone trails that guide subsequent ants. The trail IS the coordination mechanism. Humans do the same: documentation, decisions, completed work, code commits, and audit trails become the coordination substrate that others navigate by.

Entities do not need to communicate directly if the environment carries enough signal. A well-maintained registry tells new contributors what exists without anyone explaining it to them. A decision log tells future decision-makers what was already tried without the original decider being present. A health dashboard tells operators what needs attention without anyone filing a ticket. The environment speaks on behalf of past actors.

Well-designed governance surfaces — registries, dashboards, health scores, changelogs, decision records — enable indirect coordination at scale without bottleneck meetings or synchronization overhead. The key insight is that coordination cost does not need to scale linearly with team size if the environment is information-rich enough. Ten people can coordinate through meetings. A hundred cannot. But a hundred people can coordinate through a well-designed information environment just as easily as ten.

## Why This Matters for Governance

Every governance artifact is a pheromone trail. A standing order coordinates behavior without the author being present. A standard enables consistent output without direct supervision. A registry enables discovery without personal introductions. The quality of indirect coordination depends entirely on the quality of the traces left in the environment — which is why JOS mandates structured frontmatter, registries, and consistent formatting.

Governance systems that rely primarily on direct coordination (meetings, approvals, escalations) create bottlenecks that collapse under scale. Governance systems that invest in environmental signals (discoverable registries, clear documentation, observable health) scale without proportional coordination overhead.

## Connects To

- JOS-C06 (Documentation Discipline) — documentation is the primary mechanism for leaving coordination traces
- JOS-C14 (Ambient Discoverability) — discoverability is what makes indirect coordination possible
- JOS-P07 (Ambient Discoverability principle) — the principle that information should be findable without asking

## Provenance

Foundry "Stigmergy / Indirect Coordination" concept, reframed as a universal operations principle. Title simplified from stigmergy to indirect coordination for accessibility. Rooted in entomology (Grasse, 1959) and wiki/open-source collaboration theory.
